messages.author: Theen
messages.text: I want to make a program for playing basic games over meshcore. I got it working for meshtastic but messages were too unreliable. First I'm going to make battleship. I originally made it in python sending and receiving messages through serial. But since meshcore can't do Bluetooth and serial at the same time (right?) I need to find something pretty easy and reliable for doing this. For messages I'm thinking ASCII readable game names like "BS" then 16 bit random game session id, and 16 bit packet counter to prevent duplicates, then payload. My thought at the moment is making it web based where it connects over serial to your meshcore on chrome based browsers and maybe Bluetooth as well to send messages for game updates in a channel #mesh-game. But this will have compatibility issues, so I'm thinking that the game also has a pastable chat for you to send in case it can't link to your node that you send in the channel and paste updates as well. Make it base 64 payloads to keep it paste safe. Any thoughts for maximizing convenience and reliability?
messages.content_html: I want to make a program for playing basic games over meshcore. I got it working for meshtastic but messages were too unreliable. First I'm going to make battleship. I originally made it in python sending and receiving messages through serial. But since meshcore can't do Bluetooth and serial at the same time (right?) I need to find something pretty easy and reliable for doing this. For messages I'm thinking ASCII readable game names like "BS" then 16 bit random game session id, and 16 bit packet counter to prevent duplicates, then payload. My thought at the moment is making it web based where it connects over serial to your meshcore on chrome based browsers and maybe Bluetooth as well to send messages for game updates in a channel #mesh-game. But this will have compatibility issues, so I'm thinking that the game also has a pastable chat for you to send in case it can't link to your node that you send in the channel and paste updates as well. Make it base 64 payloads to keep it paste safe. Any thoughts for maximizing convenience and reliability?
messages.reactions_html: 2
